evidence: Test (GroundCommandHandlerTests.cs:171-180) issues TaxiCommand(path=["ZZZZZ"], DestinationRunway="28R") against MakeSimpleLayout (taxiway A only; node 3 is a 28R RunwayHoldShort joined by an edge named "A") and asserts Assert.False(result.Success) at line 179. Block .tmp/triage-blocks/TryTaxi_UnknownTaxiway_Fails.log shows Expected:False / Actual:True — V2 ACCEPTS a clearance naming a taxiway that does not exist anywhere in the layout. Trace: TryTaxi -> ResolveStandardRoute (path non-empty so NOT the A* branch) -> TaxiPathfinderV2.ResolveExplicitPath -> SegmentExpander.Run. (1) BridgeStartToTaxiway(head,"ZZZZZ") BFS finds no node carrying "ZZZZZ" -> returns ([],head). (2) Single token -> ExpandLastWaypoint -> WalkToNaturalTerminus (SegmentExpander.cs:902-997): node 1 has no edge MatchesTaxiway("ZZZZZ"), bestEdge stays null, loop breaks immediately, returns (edges=[], head, failure=null) — NO failure for the unknown taxiway. (3) Destination is Runway 28R; RouteReachesRunwayHoldShort([]) is false, so TryVariantExtension(head,"ZZZZZ","28R") runs: FindVariantHoldShorts and FindSameNameHoldShorts both look for edges named "ZZZZZ"/"ZZZZZn" and find none (the 28R hold-short edge is "A"), returning (null,null) — still no failure. (4) RouteMaterialiser.Materialise(edges=[],...) (RouteMaterialiser.cs:17-27) returns a non-null zero-segment TaxiRoute. SegmentExpander.Run returns (route, null) -> failReason=null -> ResolveExplicitPath returns the empty route -> TryTaxi falls through to CommandDispatcher.Ok -> Success=true. V1 (TaxiPathfinder.ResolveExplicitPath -> WalkTaxiway returns found=false on first taxiway -> line 362-369 sets failReason="Cannot reach taxiway ZZZZZ from current position" and returns null) correctly rejects, so the test passes under V1. This is a genuine over-permissive routing regression (V2 accepts a clearance onto a non-existent taxiway), not a route-following or assertion issue — the resolved route is empty, the command should never have succeeded.
fixHint: Make V2 hard-fail when a named (non-node-ref) waypoint matches zero nodes in the layout. Add an existence guard in SegmentExpander: check layout.GetNodesOnTaxiway(token.Name).Count == 0 for each non-node-ref WaypointToken (in ResolveWaypoints/at the top of Run, or inside ExpandSegment/WalkToNaturalTerminus when bestEdge ends up null AND no edge matched the name) and return a PathfindingFailure(FailureKind.TaxiwayNotConnected, $"Cannot find taxiway {name} in layout", ...) — mirroring the unknown-toTaxiway guard already present in TryDetour (SegmentExpander.cs:1505-1519). That propagates failReason back through ResolveExplicitPath -> TryTaxi so Success becomes false.

evidence: Assertion fails at TwoPilotControllerResponseGateE2ETests.cs:108 — Assert.NotEqual(default, secondGate): secondGate == Tuple(0, null), i.e. N436MS NEVER set the controller-response gate in the full 300s run. The block's only logs are FrequencyState (the sole enabled category): N569SX's gate fires at t=1.0 ('<none> → N569SX') and re-arms at t=125.2s and t=245.2s. That re-arming proves the gate logic works correctly: in solo mode no controller ever responds, so FrequencyState.SelectReadyIndex (FrequencyState.cs:199-217) falls through to FIFO after AwaitedTimeoutSeconds=8s and lets N569SX re-transmit — it would equally release any queued N436MS Proactive within 8s. So the pilot-speech/gate layer is NOT the bug; N436MS simply never produced its holding-short 'ready for departure' Proactive. That Proactive is queued only inside HoldingShortPhase.OnStart (HoldingShortPhase.cs:60-79, via BuildHoldingShortReady) when the aircraft enters HoldingShortPhase with a runway reason — i.e. only after N436MS completes its preset `TAXI B 28R` (C182 from coords lat 37.725646/lon -122.204694, hdg 133) and ArriveAtNode inserts HoldingShortPhase at the 28R DestinationRunway hold-short (TaxiingPhase.cs:348). It never arrived in 300s. `TAXI B 28R` is a single-named-taxiway-direct-to-runway route (B is a real OAK taxiway, 28R a real runway) — exactly the route class hardened by landed fixes #1 (DestinationRunway reason) and req ① (single-name beats membership), so a routing reject/missing-route is unlikely; the documented V2 regressions on OAK piston taxi-outs are on the FOLLOWING side (navigator.md:200-221: AMX669 freeze, orbit stalls, synth-tolerance vs tight V2 arcs, proximity-stop deadlock), and the sibling N436MS_TaxiC_AtJSX1_HasNoReversals block name confirms N436MS taxi-following reversals are a tracked navigator concern. Most probable: N436MS spins/stalls/freezes while following the B route under V2 and never reaches the hold-short.

evidence: Expected CurrentPhase=InitialClimbPhase; Actual=TaxiingPhase. The test diagnostic prints `phase=Taxiing alt=9 ias=0 route=[]` at t=435 — alt=9/ias=0 is the exact spawn state (history: t=0 SPAWN pos=37.7324/-122.2155 hdg=135 alt=9), so the aircraft never moved off its parking spot. N172SP spawns at OAK parking GA7 (node 642) with preset `TAXI D C B 28R` at t=0 (scenario --show presets/spawns). Under V1 (recording capture) this taxi succeeded: history shows t=215 PHASES rebuilt Taxiing->[HoldingShort]->HoldingInPosition, then t=320 CTO TRDCT OAK30NUM 014 -> LineUp->Takeoff->InitialClimb, t=395 InitialClimb. Under V2+V2 (ship target) none of that happens — the aircraft sits in TaxiingPhase, so the CTO never takes effect and InitialClimb/ADCT are never reached. The InitialClimb ADCT fix itself is already landed (InitialClimbPhase.cs:309/320 returns Allowed for AppendDirectTo), so the asserted contract would pass if the aircraft reached InitialClimb. Topology confirms a valid route exists under V2: LayoutInspector --fillet-mode v2 --node 642 shows GA7->1238 via RAMP, node 1238 has D-RAMP arcs to 1236/1237 (reaches taxiway D); taxiways D(115 nodes)/C(105)/B(88) all present and B intersects 28R at HS 186/188 (--exits 28R via B). evidence: The RESOLVED explicit route itself contains a physical hairpin, not a following artifact. BearingFlipPenalty (TaxiPathfinder.cs:111/1110) is only summed inside RouteCost (:1088) which feeds the A* FindRoute, never the explicit-walk look-ahead. evidence: Contract (SfoRampCrossesRunwayTests.cs:42-83): TAXI A E 28R HS E to gated N70234 must FAIL because reaching taxiway A from the gate requires a straight-line RAMP segment across two runways. evidence: The test (tests/Yaat.Sim.Tests/TaxiPathfinderTests.cs:2272-2278) identifies the SFO A1 apex same-taxiway arc by `arc.Nodes.All(n => n.Origin?.Contains("@507") == true)`. The `@507` substring is the LEGACY tangent-node origin format: TangentNodeProvenance.DisplayString = "Fillet:tangent-node@{IntersectionId} ..." (FilletProvenance.cs:20) → "...@507...". V2 materializes tangent-cut nodes with Origin = $"V2:tangent-cut@J{cut.JunctionNodeId}/..." (FilletPlanExecutor.cs:190) → "V2:tangent-cut@J507/A1". `"...@J507...".Contains("@507")` is FALSE (the `J` prefix breaks the substring), so the first assertion at line 2278 fails. V2's actual geometry is correct: at the A1 apex SharesTaxiway is true, so V2 emits a single-name corner arc with TaxiwayNames=["A1"] (FilletPlanExecutor.cs:106,111) satisfying the first two clauses of the `usesArc` predicate, and junction 507 is in JunctionNodesToRemove (FilletPlanExecutor.cs:139-144) so the route never visits node 507 (the second assertion, line 2281, holds). Cuts only redirect to STABLE surviving nodes (FilletPlanCutRedirect.cs:12-20,29-87); the removed junction 507 is not a redirect target, so endpoints stay tangent cuts tagged @J507. The test's own comment (lines 2269-2271) intended an implementation-neutral identifier but coupled to the Legacy origin string. The contract — 'M2→A→A1 route uses the same-taxiway A1 arc and skips node 507' — is met by V2; only the origin-substring matcher is V1-pinned.
fixHint: Relax the arc identifier in the `usesArc` predicate (TaxiPathfinderTests.cs:2272-2276) so it matches both generators: change `n.Origin?.Contains("@507") == true` to also accept the V2 form, e.g. `(n.Origin?.Contains("@507") == true) || (n.Origin?.Contains("@J507") == true)`. Better/robust: drop the origin-string coupling entirely and identify by single-name TaxiwayNames==["A1"] plus geometric proximity of the arc nodes' SourceIntersectionPosition to the original junction-507 position. The second assertion (visitsApex==false) needs no change.
